title: Swallenia alexandrae
taxonomy: Swallenia alexandrae is a species of flowering plant in the family Poaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States, particularly in the state of Nevada. As a member of the Poaceae family, Swallenia alexandrae is classified as a type of grass. The plant's classification is further specified as a monocot, with its seeds and leaves displaying characteristics of this group. Swallenia alexandrae is often grouped within the subfamily Chloridoideae.

intro: Swallenia alexandrae, also known as Alexandra's Desert Heath, is a rare and endangered shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and protect against cell damage.
